summary.intendedStageSupportPolicy: runtime promotion requires no expected-label regression unless the expected label remains the best match and target-video fit improves by >=0.55/10 with no more than -0.15/10 expected drift. It also requires the best trajectory match to be one of the expected stage labels, an expected-label tie within 0.05/10 with >=0.3 expected lift and >=0.15 target-video lift, or a strong expected-label score >=7 with >=0.5 expected lift, >=0.8 target-video lift, and an identity margin no worse than -0.35/10. Human-perfect potential must not regress versus baseline because challenge-stage conformance is not useful if a strong player loses readable scoring windows. For late challenge stages, candidates whose best match belongs to the wrong Galaga challenge number are explicitly penalized and blocked from promotion.
summary.playerMeaning: This pass improved the search process more than the shipped game: no candidate earned enough measured lift to promote safely.
summary.processMeaning: Future challenge tuning can now compare many runtime candidates against Galaga labels before editing game constants.
summary.nextStep: Use target-trajectory controls and richer reference labels before changing runtime stage-3 gameplay.

measurementPolicy.reference: media-backed Galaga challenge labels with comparison vectors
measurementPolicy.promotionRule: Require the expected challenge-label identity to be the best match or tied within 0.05/10 with evidence on both expected and target-video axes, no safety regression, no human-perfect potential regression, no matching full-analyzer rejection, and at least +0.35/10 expected-label or target-video lift over baseline before runtime promotion. A material target-video win may tolerate up to -0.15/10 expected drift only when expected identity remains the best match. Strong non-best candidates must be very close on identity margin and improve both expected and target-video scores. If a prior full-analyzer review rejected a similar or identical candidate, the sweep must clear that calibration by a material margin before it can be marked ready again.
measurementPolicy.safety: Reject candidates with enemy shots, enemy attack starts, ship losses, or lower human-perfect potential in the challenge window.
